_metadata.title: MFA Effectiveness Testing Results — Q1 2026
_metadata.test_conducted_by: Internal Security Team — Red Team Exercise

test_objectives: Verify MFA enforcement for all admin accounts
Test bypass attempts against conditional access policies
Verify phishing-resistant MFA effectiveness against simulated attacks
Assess MFA fatigue attack resilience (notification bombing)

test_scenarios.name: Admin Account MFA Bypass Attempt
test_scenarios.description: Attempt to authenticate as admin without MFA
test_scenarios.detail: 3 admin accounts (usr_admin_004, usr_admin_006, usr_admin_010) authenticated without MFA challenge — no CA policy applied

test_scenarios.name: Standard User MFA Bypass Attempt
test_scenarios.description: Attempt to authenticate as standard user without MFA
test_scenarios.detail: 38% of tested standard accounts had no MFA requirement — consistent with 62% MFA enrollment rate

test_scenarios.name: MFA Notification Fatigue Attack
test_scenarios.description: Send repeated push notifications to test user acceptance
test_scenarios.detail: Number matching on Microsoft Authenticator effectively prevents notification fatigue attacks

test_scenarios.name: Legacy Authentication Protocol Attack
test_scenarios.description: Attempt to authenticate via legacy protocols (IMAP, POP3, Basic Auth)
test_scenarios.detail: 7 service accounts authenticated via legacy protocols — these have exceptions in Block-Legacy-Auth policy

test_scenarios.name: FIDO2 Phishing Resistance Test
test_scenarios.description: Simulated phishing attack against FIDO2-protected admin accounts
test_scenarios.detail: FIDO2 security keys are fully phishing-resistant — no successful phishing attacks against FIDO2-protected accounts

overall_results.summary: MFA controls are partially effective. Strong enforcement exists for most admin accounts and phishing-resistant methods work well. Key gaps: 3 admin accounts lack MFA enforcement, 38% of standard users have no MFA, and some legacy auth bypasses exist for service accounts.
